locations.name: The Crossroads
locations.description_template: A quiet crossroads where four narrow roads meet. A weathered stone wall lines the eastern side, half-hidden by brambles. The {weather} sky stretches over the flat midlands. It is {time}.

connections.path_description: a short lane past a row of cottages
a winding boreen lined with hawthorn
the road past a hand-lettered sign
a muddy path along the schoolmaster's wall
a narrow road between stone walls
a few steps across the road
the Kilteevan road heading south past low fields

locations.mythological_significance: Crossroads hold power in Irish folklore — a place between places, where the veil is thin.

locations.name: Darcy's Pub
locations.description_template: The warm interior of Darcy's Pub. Turf smoke hangs in the air. Old prints of hurling matches line the walls beside a faded map of the county. A fiddle sits propped in the corner. It is {time} and the weather outside is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the lane back to the crossroads

locations.name: St. Brigid's Church
locations.description_template: A small stone church with a slate roof, its stained-glass windows catching the {time} light. The graveyard behind is thick with Celtic crosses and lichen-covered headstones. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the boreen back to the crossroads
a muddy track past the graveyard wall

locations.mythological_significance: Built on the site of an older holy well dedicated to Brigid, the goddess-turned-saint.

locations.name: The Letter Office
locations.description_template: A small stone building where the mail coach leaves correspondence for the parish. A hand-lettered sign reads 'Letters' above the door. Notices and handbills flutter on the board outside. It is {time}. The weather is {weather}.

connections.path_description: back across to the crossroads

locations.name: The Hurling Green
locations.description_template: An open field where men play hurling on fair days. Goalposts of ash wood stand at each end. Stone ditches mark the boundary, and a few spectators' stools sit along the edge. The {weather} sky hangs over the flat green. It is {time}.

connections.path_description: a beaten path back past the schoolmaster's cabin

locations.name: The Hedge School
locations.description_template: A low thatched cabin where the schoolmaster teaches Latin, Irish, and arithmetic to the children of the parish. A rough bench sits outside where classes are held in fine weather. It is {time}. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the muddy path back to the crossroads
a beaten path down to the hurling green

locations.name: Lough Ree Shore
locations.description_template: The stony shore of Lough Ree stretches out before you. The great lake shimmers under the {weather} sky. Small islands dot the water in the distance. It is {time}.

connections.path_description: a lakeside path through reeds and willow
a rough track climbing away from the shore

locations.mythological_significance: Lough Ree is said to be home to a monster — the Lough Ree wurm, Ireland's lesser-known cousin of Nessie.

locations.name: Hodson Bay
locations.description_template: A sheltered bay on Lough Ree. Boats bob at their moorings. The old boathouse leans into the wind. It is {time} and the sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the lakeside path back along the shore
a lane winding uphill from the bay

locations.name: Murphy's Farm
locations.description_template: A working farm with a whitewashed house and stone outbuildings roofed in thatch. Cattle graze in the near field. A sheepdog watches from the gate. It is {time}. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the narrow road back between stone walls
a lane winding down toward the bay
a boreen through a gap in the hedge

locations.name: O'Brien's Farm
locations.description_template: A tidy farm with stone outbuildings and a well-kept garden. Hens scratch in the yard. A donkey stands patiently by the barn. It is {time}. Weather: {weather}.

connections.path_description: a boreen back through the hedge to Murphy's
a soggy track across the lower field

locations.name: The Fairy Fort
locations.description_template: An ancient ring fort — a raised circular mound ringed by hawthorn and elder. The air feels different here, heavy and watchful. It is {time}. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the rough track back down to the shore
a barely visible path through gorse and bracken

locations.mythological_significance: A rath said to be home to the sídhe. No farmer has ever ploughed within twenty yards of it. Those who disturb fairy forts are said to suffer terrible misfortune.

locations.name: The Bog Road
locations.description_template: A rough track cutting through blanket bog. Turf banks stand in neat rows, drying in the wind. The {weather} sky presses down. Pools of dark water gleam between the heather. It is {time}.

connections.path_description: the muddy track back toward the church
a barely visible path through gorse to the fort
a squelching path along the bog's edge

locations.mythological_significance: Bogs preserve everything — bodies, butter, memories. People say you can hear voices in the wind here on certain nights.

locations.name: Connolly's Shop
locations.description_template: A small general shop crammed with everything from spades and tallow candles to bolts of cloth and bags of meal. The bell above the door jangles. It is {time}. Outside, the weather is {weather}.

connections.path_description: a few steps back across the road to the crossroads

locations.name: The Lime Kiln
locations.description_template: A squat stone lime kiln standing at the edge of the bog. On burning days the smoke rises thick and white. A flat patch of ground beside it serves as a gathering place. It is {time}. The weather is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the soggy track back up to O'Brien's
a path along the bog's edge back to the road

locations.name: Kilteevan Village
locations.description_template: The small village of Kilteevan — a handful of whitewashed cottages clustered around a well and an old stone bridge over a shallow stream. Smoke drifts from chimneys. A rooster crows from behind a low wall. The {weather} sky hangs over the quiet street. It is {time}.

connections.path_description: the road north past low fields to the crossroads

locations.mythological_significance: Kilteevan — Cill Tíobáin, the church of St. Tíobán. The ruins of the old church stand in a field nearby, half-swallowed by ivy and elder.
